proposed_hypotheses.text: Older patients (higher feature_011) have a lower objective_response rate than younger patients in this AML cohort.
Increasing feature_004 (ordinal 0/1/2, plausibly an ECOG-like performance status) is associated with monotonically lower objective_response.
Higher feature_024 (continuous, range 20-100, plausibly a percent disease-burden marker such as bone-marrow blast fraction) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Across feature_011 quartiles (medians ~57, 65, 71, 78), ORR is 0.186 / 0.194 / 0.182 / 0.187 -- essentially flat, no main effect of age-like feature.
ORR by feature_004: level 0 ORR=0.231 (n=17592), level 1 ORR=0.174 (n=24971), level 2 ORR=0.129 (n=7437). Strong monotone negative trend; logistic per-unit coef = -0.36 (p<1e-50).
ORR by feature_024 quartiles: 0.207 / 0.194 / 0.183 / 0.166. Clear monotone decrease with higher values; logistic coef per std = -0.10 (p<1e-15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (binary, ~40% prevalence) is associated with higher objective_response.
feature_021 (binary, ~40% prevalence) is associated with higher objective_response.
feature_014 (binary, ~30% prevalence) is associated with higher objective_response.
feature_020 (continuous, range 0-23.6) is associated with lower objective_response when high.

analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0 ORR=0.165 (n=29944); feature_001=1 ORR=0.221 (n=20056). Marginal log-odds-ratio +0.36, chi2 p<1e-50.
feature_021=0 ORR=0.166 (n=29980); feature_021=1 ORR=0.220 (n=20020). Marginal log-odds-ratio +0.35, chi2 p<1e-50.
feature_014=0 ORR=0.167 (n=34986); feature_014=1 ORR=0.235 (n=15014). Marginal log-odds-ratio +0.42, chi2 p<1e-50.
ORR by feature_020 tertiles (qcut to 4 collapses): 0.204 / 0.186 / 0.156. Logistic coef per std = -0.14 (p<1e-30).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, feature_001, feature_021, and feature_014 each remain independently and positively associated with objective_response, while feature_004, feature_020, and feature_024 each remain independently and negatively associated.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable additive logistic regression of objective_response on all 35 features (continuous standardized). Significant adjusted coefficients: feature_014 +0.44, feature_001 +0.37, feature_021 +0.36 (all p<1e-50); feature_004 -0.36; feature_020 -0.14; feature_024 -0.10; feature_009 -0.19; feature_026 -0.17 (all p<1e-5). All other features had |coef|<0.06 and p>0.05 except feature_022 (-0.05), feature_027 (-0.06), feature_029 (+0.06).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 and feature_021 interact positively: their joint presence (feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1) yields a higher objective_response than expected from the sum of their marginal effects.
feature_001 and feature_014 interact positively: their joint presence (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1) yields a higher objective_response than expected from the sum of their marginal effects.
feature_021 and feature_014 interact positively: their joint presence (feature_021=1 AND feature_014=1) yields a higher objective_response than expected from the sum of their marginal effects.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by (feature_001, feature_021): (0,0)=0.168, (0,1)=0.160, (1,0)=0.163, (1,1)=0.308. Difference-in-differences interaction = +0.153 (p<1e-50). The +5pp marginal effects of either feature alone disappear when the other is absent and combine to a +14pp jump only when both =1.
ORR by (feature_001, feature_014): (0,0)=0.168, (0,1)=0.159, (1,0)=0.166, (1,1)=0.349. Difference-in-differences interaction = +0.193 (p<1e-50).
ORR by (feature_021, feature_014): (0,0)=0.169, (0,1)=0.160, (1,0)=0.165, (1,1)=0.347. Difference-in-differences interaction = +0.191 (p<1e-50).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_014=1 (the 'triple-positive' subgroup, ~4.8% of the cohort) have a markedly higher objective_response rate (~60%+) than any other combination of these three features.

feature_001, feature_021, and feature_014 are mutually statistically independent in the cohort, so the triple-positive subgroup arises by chance combination at the predicted rate (0.40 x 0.40 x 0.30 ~= 0.048).

analyses.code: Marginal P(feature_001=1)=0.401, P(feature_021=1)=0.400, P(feature_014=1)=0.300; product=0.048; observed P(triple)=2401/50000=0.0480.
analyses.result_summary: The observed triple-positive prevalence (4.80%) matches the product of marginals (4.81%) to 3 decimals. Pairwise crosstabs also factor cleanly. The three features are statistically independent in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An indicator triple_pos = (feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_014=1) added to a multivariable logistic model dominates the response signal: its coefficient is large and positive, and the individual coefficients on feature_001, feature_021, feature_014 collapse toward zero or even reverse, confirming the response is driven by their joint co-occurrence rather than three additive main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Adding triple_pos to the all-features additive logit: triple_pos coef = +2.30 (SE 0.057, OR=10.0, p<1e-300). After conditioning on triple_pos, feature_001 and feature_021 effects vanish (|coef|<0.05, p>0.5) and feature_014 even reverses to coef -0.07 (p=0.016). triple_pos absorbs essentially all of the three features' main-effect signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (n=2401), feature_026=1 (resistance-marker-like, ~15% prevalence in full cohort) drops objective_response sharply, suggesting feature_026 is a treatment/response-resistance modifier specifically active in the responsive subgroup.
Within the triple-positive subgroup, feature_009=1 (~10% prevalence in full cohort) similarly drops objective_response sharply.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive: feature_026=0 ORR=0.717 (n=2025) vs feature_026=1 ORR=0.184 (n=376). Difference -0.53 (chi2 p=7.6e-86).
Within triple-positive: feature_009=0 ORR=0.691 (n=2125) vs feature_009=1 ORR=0.188 (n=276). Difference -0.50 (chi2 p=3.3e-59).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 each interact strongly and negatively with triple_pos: their adverse effect is concentrated in (and roughly limited to) the triple-positive subgroup, while in non-triple-positive patients they have negligible main effects on ORR.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic with explicit interactions. Main effects in non-triple: feature_026 coef +0.015 (p=0.67, no effect); feature_009 coef -0.007 (p=0.86, no effect). Triple_pos main coef +3.03 (OR=20.6). Interactions: triple_pos x feature_026 = -2.74 (p<1e-50, OR=0.064); triple_pos x feature_009 = -2.59 (p<1e-50, OR=0.075). Triple_pos x feature_004 = -0.09 (p=0.25, not a strong interaction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (ordinal 0/1/2, ECOG-like) acts mainly as a global, additive negative modifier of objective_response, with no strong differential effect across the triple-positive vs non-triple-positive subgroups.
feature_020 (continuous) acts as a global negative modifier in both triple-positive and non-triple-positive subgroups.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by (triple_pos, feature_004): non-triple 0/1/2 = 0.208 / 0.152 / 0.108; triple 0/1/2 = 0.698 / 0.612 / 0.554. Roughly parallel ordinal decreases in both strata; per-unit logistic effect ~ -0.39 in non-triple, ~ -0.50 in triple. Interaction p=0.25.

feature_020 tertiles: non-triple 0.182/0.163/0.133; triple 0.651/0.628/0.603. Both strata decline with higher feature_020. Within-triple coef -0.18 (p=0.0004); within non-triple coef -0.155 (p<1e-30). Roughly parallel.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple_pos x feature_024 has a positive interaction: feature_024 is negatively associated with ORR in non-triple-positive patients but the slope is attenuated (close to zero) in triple-positive patients.
Triple_pos does not interact materially with feature_011, feature_022, feature_028, feature_007, feature_034, feature_029 (all interaction p>0.1).

analyses.result_summary: triple_pos x feature_024 (continuous, std) coef = +0.152 (p=5.6e-4). Non-triple slope -0.114, triple slope ~ +0.04. ORR by feature_024 quartile in triple: 0.634 / 0.610 / 0.649 / 0.640 (essentially flat); in non-triple: 0.185 / 0.172 / 0.160 / 0.142 (declining).

analyses.code: for cont in [...]: sm.Logit(y, X[['triple_pos', cont, 'tp_x_'+cont]]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: triple_pos x continuous-feature interactions (p): feature_011 0.38, feature_022 0.79, feature_028 0.27, feature_007 0.57, feature_034 0.38, feature_029 0.88, feature_027 0.19, feature_012 0.19, feature_020 0.24. None significant at 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the non-triple-positive cohort (n=47599), no pair of binary features (excluding feature_001/021/014) defines a comparably large response-elevation cell (i.e., no hidden second 'responder' subgroup with ORR >= 0.30 driven by a binary x binary interaction).

analyses.code: Pair scan: for each pair (f1,f2) of binary features other than 001/021/014, compute ORR in (f1=1,f2=1) cell within triple_pos==0 and flag any cell with n>=50 and ORR>0.30.
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise binary-x-binary scan within non-triple-positive cohort produced no cell with ORR > 0.30 and n >= 50. The only interaction-driven response-elevation in this cohort is the one involving feature_001 x feature_021 x feature_014.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete best-supported response-defining subgroup is: feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0. Patients meeting all five predicates have ORR ~78.6% vs ~16.5% in everyone else (>4x higher), and removing any single predicate substantially lowers the subgroup's ORR.

Layering feature_004=0 (best ECOG-like value) on top further raises ORR to ~85.9% (n=617), but this is a global ordinal effect of feature_004 rather than a triple_pos-specific modifier, so feature_004 is best treated as an additive prognostic factor rather than part of the core treatment-effect-defining subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Five-feature subgroup: n=1790, ORR=0.786. Comparator (all others, n=48210): ORR=0.165. Fisher OR = 18.55, p<1e-300. Sensitivity (one constraint at a time relaxed): drop feature_001=1 -> ORR 0.403; drop feature_021=1 -> 0.406; drop feature_014=1 -> 0.349; drop feature_026=0 -> 0.691; drop feature_009=0 -> 0.717. All five constraints contribute and are necessary for the maximal effect.

Adding feature_004=0 to the five-feature definition: n=617, ORR=0.859. The incremental ORR rise (0.786 -> 0.859) is consistent with the ~7-percentage-point gradient feature_004 produces in any stratum, not a unique triple_pos x feature_004 interaction (interaction p=0.25). So feature_004=0 is best regarded as an additional prognostic favorable factor rather than part of the minimal subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: If feature_001 (or feature_021 or feature_014) is interpreted as the 'treatment received' indicator in this AML cohort and the others as required biomarkers, then the treatment effect on objective_response is essentially zero in the absence of either biomarker (-0.5 to +0.5 percentage points) and ~+18 percentage points (absolute) in the biomarker-positive subgroup, with feature_026 and feature_009 abolishing that benefit entirely. Equivalent statements hold under any of the three labelings, since the three features are statistically independent and enter the joint effect symmetrically.

analyses.code: If feature_001 is treatment: ORR(trt=1 | bm21=1 & bm14=1 & f026=0 & f009=0) - ORR(trt=0 | bm21=1 & bm14=1 & f026=0 & f009=0)
analyses.result_summary: Treating feature_001 as the treatment, restricting to feature_021=1 & feature_014=1 & feature_026=0 & feature_009=0: ORR with feature_001=1 (n=1790) = 0.786 vs feature_001=0 (n=2654) = 0.150. Treatment effect = +0.636 (absolute). Outside this biomarker set, ORR(feature_001=1) - ORR(feature_001=0) = (0.165 - 0.166) = -0.001. The huge response heterogeneity is driven entirely by the joint biomarker-and-no-suppressor subgroup. Under the symmetric labelings (treating feature_021 or feature_014 as the treatment), the same magnitude (~+0.63 in subgroup, ~0 outside) is recovered, since the three features are independent and interchangeable in the joint indicator.
